evidence_titles: Anthropic is paying $15 billion a year for access to Elon Musk’s data centers
evidence_summaries: Earlier this month, SpaceX and Anthropic announced a new compute partnership that provides access to the rocket company's Colossus data centers in Memphis, TN. Now, with the release of SpaceX's IPO filing, we have more details about that deal, including how much Anthropic is paying to Elon Musk's company. In its S-1 filing, SpaceX said […]

worth_tracking.title: OpenAI：OpenAI's Yann Dubois: Why AI Progress Suddenly Feels Real
worth_tracking.brief: OpenAI's Yann Dubois: Why AI Progress Suddenly Feels Real。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
evidence_titles: OpenAI's Yann Dubois: Why AI Progress Suddenly Feels Real
evidence_summaries: Speaker 1 | 00:00 - 00:26 You need to reach this level of reliability to really make any of these AI tools very useful, and I think we just crossed that probably December last year, at least at OpenAI. Now we can trust these models to do a lot of the work that we are doing. The last few months have been pretty wild. We moved from like competitions to usefulness to users and that's what we are feeling right now. I think most of the time the Bionic is the last mile. Speaker 1 | 00:26 - 00:34 There will always be a lot of space left for this last mile in different verticals. And I would highly encourage people to continue working on that. Speaker 2 | 00:34 - 01:03 Hi, I'm Matt Turk. Welcome to the MAD Podcast. My guest today is Jan Dubois, who co leads the post training Frontiers team at OpenAI. The recent release of GBT 5.5 was yet another major milestone in AI, and Jan's team helped build it alongside OpenAI's prior top reasoning models, including o three and GBT five thinking. Before OpenAI, Jan was at Stanford, where he co authored Stanford Alpaca, the landmark project that kicked off much of the modern post training research community. Speaker 2 | 01:03 - 01:29 In this convers

items.title: TransitLM: A Large-Scale Dataset and Benchmark for Map-Free Transit Route Generation
items.title_zh: TransitLM: A Large-Scale Dataset and Benchmark for Map-Free Transit Route Generation
items.takeaway_zh: 《TransitLM: A Large-Scale Dataset and Benchmark for Map-Free Transit Route Generation》是当天 Hugging Face Papers 中值得放入技术雷达的一篇。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 它可能影响 Agent 训练、推理策略或任务自动化能力。

items.title: Evaluating Proactive Personal Assistant Agents in Long-Horizon Workflows
items.title_zh: Evaluating Proactive Personal Assistant Agents in Long-Horizon Workflows
items.takeaway_zh: 《Evaluating Proactive Personal Assistant Agents in Long-Horizon Workflows》讨论 Agent 工作流中的缓存、规划执行和效率优化。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 它可能影响 Agent 训练、推理策略或任务自动化能力。

items.title: Discriminative Token Credit Assignment for Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards
items.title_zh: Discriminative Token Credit Assignment for Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards
items.takeaway_zh: 《Discriminative Token Credit Assignment for Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards》围绕推理过程建模，探索更强的递归式问题求解。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 它可能影响 Agent 训练、推理策略或任务自动化能力。

items.title: Perception or Prejudice: Can MLLMs Go Beyond First Impressions of Personality?
items.title_zh: Perception or Prejudice: Can MLLMs Go Beyond First Impressions of Personality?
items.takeaway_zh: 《Perception or Prejudice: Can MLLMs Go Beyond First Impressions of Personality?》讨论 Agent 工作流中的缓存、规划执行和效率优化。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 它可能影响 Agent 训练、推理策略或任务自动化能力。

The recent release of GBT 5.5 was yet another major milestone in AI, and Jan's team helped build it alongside OpenAI's prior top reasoning models, including o three and GBT five thinking. Before OpenAI, Jan was at Stanford, where he co authored Stanford Alpaca, the landmark project that kicked off much of the modern post training research community.
